scenario: Lattice Bank runs Camunda 8.8 with 3500 active payment instances in Operate. One instance is stuck mid-flow after a misrouted decision and the operator needs to advance that single instance past the stuck activity, allowing its execution to continue while the other 3499 instances keep running untouched.
question: How does process instance modification in Operate let the operator handle the single stuck instance?

options.text: Process instance modification lets the operator continue execution by skipping or repeating activities on the one stuck instance, without altering any of the other running payment instances
Process instance batch modification is the only available path, so the operator selects every running payment instance and moves all of them to the same next activity in one operation
Operate signals the broker to automatically push the stuck token forward to the next healthy flow node, with no operator action required on the affected instance
Operate cannot advance a stuck instance, so the operator must cancel it and create a brand new process instance from scratch to keep processing the underlying payment work

a.text: Correct. The docs describe process instance modification as the mechanism to allow execution to continue on a stuck instance, where the operator can skip or repeat activities for that single instance without affecting any others.

b.text: Incorrect. Batch modification is intended for the separate case where many instances entered the wrong branch and need to move together; using it here would alter healthy payment instances unintentionally.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs describe an operator-driven modification mode in Operate; the broker does not silently advance stuck tokens to a healthy flow node on its own.

d.text: Incorrect. Operate offers modification mode precisely so that a stuck instance can continue execution by skipping or repeating activities, avoiding the need to cancel and recreate the instance.

explanation: Process instance modification in Operate is the operator action that lets a single stuck instance continue execution by skipping or repeating activities. Batch modification is a separate operation for cases where many instances entered the wrong branch and need to be moved together.
